 {"id":"phage_explorer-7gs","content_hash":"87dce5476d989d9397aee417d9ca2804db7dfd24ff89715603ff6af43f90f06e","title":"Advanced: Functional Module Coherence & Stoichiometry Checker","description":"Segment genomes into functional modules (replication, morphogenesis, lysis). Evaluate stoichiometric balance (capsid:scaffold ratios). Flag incomplete or overrepresented modules, suggest missing partners. Module ribbon with green/amber/red indicators. Quick genome QC and design check. Novelty: Medium-high.","notes":"## FUNCTIONAL MODULE COHERENCE & STOICHIOMETRY CHECKER

**Full TypeScript Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Perform comprehensive 'genome health check' by segmenting into functional modules and evaluating completeness, balance, and coherence.

### Mathematical Foundations

**Module Completeness Score:**
```
C_module = Σ(w_i × present_i) / Σ(w_i)

where:
- w_i: weight (1.0=critical, 0.5=common, 0.2=optional)
- present_i: 1 if gene found, 0 otherwise
```

**Stoichiometric Balance Score:**
```
imbalance_ij = |log2(observed_i / observed_j) - log2(r_ij)|
S_balance = 1 - tanh(Σ imbalance_ij / n_pairs)
```

**Module Coherence (Gene Neighborhood):**
```
Coherence = Σ(adjacent_same_module) / (Σ genes_in_module - 1)
```

**Expression Proxy from RBS:**
```
RBS_strength = PSSM_score(Shine-Dalgarno, -15 to -5)
Expected_copies ∝ RBS_strength × CAI
```

### Complete TypeScript Implementation

- `ModuleType`: replication, morphogenesis_head/tail, packaging, lysis, lysogeny, host_interaction, auxiliary
- `ModuleGene` with role, essentiality, expected copy number, RBS strength
- `FunctionalModule` with completeness, balance, coherence scores
- `StoichiometryRule` defining gene pair ratios
- `ModuleAnalysis` with quality grade A-F
- `MODULE_KEYWORDS` regex patterns per module type
- `ESSENTIAL_GENES` weighted lists per module
- `STOICHIOMETRY_RULES` (MCP:portal, terminase large:small, etc.)
- `estimateRBSStrength()` Shine-Dalgarno PSSM scoring
- `classifyGene()`, `determineEssentiality()`
- `assembleModules()` gene classification pipeline
- `computeCompletenessScore()`, `computeBalanceScore()`
- `computeCoherenceScore()` gene adjacency analysis
- `analyzeModuleCoherence()` main analysis function
- `renderModuleRibbon()`, `renderStoichiometryChart()`

### TUI Features
- Module Health Overview with progress bars per module
- Status indicators: ● complete, ◐ partial, ○ missing
- Missing essential gene warnings
- Stoichiometry panel per module showing:
  - Gene expression levels (bars)
  - Expected vs observed comparison
  - Balance score and coherence %
- Suggestions panel with priority levels
- Quality grade (A-F) prominently displayed
- [Tab] Next module, [S] Stoichiometry, [H] Find homologs

### Module Categories
- Replication: polymerase, helicase, primase, SSB
- Head: MCP, portal, scaffold, protease
- Tail: major tail, tape measure, fiber, baseplate
- Packaging: large/small terminase
- Lysis: endolysin, holin, spanin
- Lysogeny: integrase, CI, excisionase

 {"id":"phage_explorer-8mgm","content_hash":"e4ac95e4f61be62dc8507bbb13263c90e53d8f247f06d5f18cf2567e688b971e","title":"Feature: Amino Acid View UX Enhancements","description":"","notes":"# Feature: Amino Acid View UX Enhancements

## Executive Summary

Improve the amino acid view experience for non-specialists by using clear terminology, prominent controls, and rich contextual information.

## Current Problems

1. **Confusing Abbreviation**: "AA" means nothing to non-biologists
2. **Subtle Toggle**: DNA/AA toggle is too small and similar to other controls
3. **No Context**: Tapping an amino acid provides no educational information

## User Stories

> As a student viewing a phage protein sequence,
> I want to understand what each amino acid letter means,
> So I can learn about protein structure while exploring.

> As a mobile user,
> I want clearly visible controls that are easy to tap,
> So I can switch between DNA and protein views without frustration.

## Sub-Tasks

### P1 - High Priority
- `phage_explorer-elbv`: Rename "AA" to "Amino Acids" throughout UI
- `phage_explorer-necy`: Implement touch HUD with detailed amino acid information

### P2 - Normal Priority
- `phage_explorer-fv8u`: Redesign DNA/Amino Acid toggle as prominent segmented control

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Terminology Update
Replace all instances of "AA" with "Amino Acids" in user-facing text:
- Toggle button label
- Accessibility labels
- Help text and tooltips
- Footer hints

### 2. Touch HUD
When user taps and holds on an amino acid:
- Full name (e.g., "Methionine")
- Molecular weight
- Classification (hydrophobic, polar, etc.)
- Essential/non-essential status
- Energy cost to synthesize
- Codons that encode it

### 3. Prominent Toggle
Replace small button with segmented control:
- Clear DNA / Amino Acids segments
- Visual indication of active mode
- 44px minimum touch target
- Icons for quick recognition

## File Locations

- Sequence view: `/packages/web/src/components/SequenceView.tsx`
- Amino acid data: Need to create `/packages/core/src/data/amino-acid-database.ts`

## Success Criteria

- [ ] No "AA" abbreviations in user-facing UI
- [ ] Toggle button is visually prominent and touch-friendly
- [ ] Touch HUD provides comprehensive amino acid information
- [ ] All 20 amino acids have complete data
- [ ] Scientific accuracy verified

## Definition of Done

All sub-tasks completed, tested on mobile devices, and accessibility verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:52.644495-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:34:11.629685-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-8mgm","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-jjq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.47298-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

### Concept
Infer ecological niches and co-occurring taxa from metagenomic abundance tables; derive niche vectors and networks.

### How to Build
- **Input**: BIOM/TSV + sample metadata
- **Correlations**: SparCC/FlashWeave-like compositional inference in Rust+WASM (ndarray + rayon)
- **Niche factors**: NMF/PMF in TS for latent "niche vectors"
- **Metadata mapping**: Attach habitats/hosts to factors; cache per phage
- **UI**: Co-occurrence network colored by niches; stacked niche bars per phage; filters by habitat

### Why It's Good
Adds real-world context to host predictions; complements CRISPR/prophage evidence.

### Implementation Stack
Rust+WASM for correlations; TS NMF; SQLite cache of niche vectors; BIOM parser in TS.

### TUI Visualization
Network with niche colors; press 'n' to repaint bars; tooltips show top co-occurring taxa/habitats; toggle significance threshold live.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-d2p","content_hash":"3cf931099db9f603294bd3c5240ba226a6ca796952f92df60aded7885a1f22dc","title":"Advanced: Regulatory Signal Constellations","description":"Scan promoters/terminators/RBS/operators; render co-occurring motifs as constellations. PWMs + co-occurrence graphs. Evidence score combining spacing, orientation, strength into operon-likelihood. Starfield strip above genes; motifs as glyphs; toggle inferred operons. Novelty: Medium, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## REGULATORY SIGNAL CONSTELLATIONS

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Scan promoters/terminators/RBS/operators and render co-occurring regulatory motifs as 'constellations' to reveal operons and control logic.

### How to Build

**Motif Scans:**
- PWMs for sigma factors (σ70, σ32, σ54, σS)
- Rho-independent terminator detection (stem-loop + poly-U)
- RBS (Shine-Dalgarno) scoring
- Operator palindrome detection
- Run in TS (fast) or Rust+WASM for batch

**Co-occurrence Graph:**
- Build small graph of motifs per region
- Detect motif 'motifs' (regulatory patterns):
  - Promoter + operator + terminator spacing
  - Dual promoters (divergent)
  - Antitermination signals

**Evidence Score:**
- Combine spacing, orientation, and strength
- Operon-likelihood per gene cluster
- Regulatory complexity score

**Link to Genes:**
- Tie regulatory regions to downstream ORFs
- Annotate likely operons and regulons

### TUI Features
- Starfield strip above genes (motifs as glyphs)
- Edges show spacing relationships
- Hover shows PWM score for each motif
- Press 'o' to toggle inferred operon highlighting
- ORF blocks colored by regulatory context
- Legend showing motif glyph meanings

### Why It's Good
- Surfaces regulatory architecture beyond genes
- Shows how multiple motifs compose control logic
- Aids understanding of temporal expression
- Spacing and phase matter for function

### Implementation Stack
- TS PWM scanning
- Optional Rust+WASM for speed on large genomes
- SQLite motif hit cache
- Ink starfield rendering with braille and symbols

 {"id":"phage_explorer-mkpk","content_hash":"1fb228243590459764bc2c63366c321905e8aed942b37942ced603641587d019","title":"Phase 6: 3D Visualization","description":"## Overview

Implement WebGL-based 3D protein structure visualization to replace the TUI's ASCII 3D rendering.

## Why 3D Matters

The TUI has an ASCII 3D model viewer that renders protein structures using characters. This is impressive for terminal art but limited in:
- Resolution and detail
- Smooth rotation/zoom
- Surface rendering modes
- Lighting and depth perception

WebGL enables true 3D visualization with:
- Smooth rotation via mouse/touch
- Multiple rendering modes (ribbon, surface, ball-and-stick)
- Real-time lighting
- High-resolution detail

## Technical Components

### 1. WebGL Model Viewer

**Technology options**:
- **Three.js** - Full-featured 3D library, large but capable
- **BabylonJS** - Alternative, similar capabilities
- **Raw WebGL** - Smallest bundle, most control, most work

**Recommendation**: Three.js with tree-shaking for reasonable bundle size

**Features**:
- Load PDB/mmCIF structure files
- Multiple render modes:
  - Ribbon (secondary structure)
  - Surface (solvent-accessible)
  - Ball-and-stick (atomic detail)
  - Cartoon (simplified)
- Color schemes:
  - By element
  - By residue type
  - By chain
  - By B-factor
  - Custom gradients
- Controls:
  - Orbit (rotate around center)
  - Pan (translate view)
  - Zoom (scroll wheel)
  - Reset (return to default view)

### 2. Fullscreen Mode

**What**: Dedicated fullscreen view for detailed structure exploration

**Features**:
- Full viewport rendering
- Keyboard controls (arrow keys for rotation)
- Quality presets (low/medium/high/ultra)
- Screenshot export
- Animation support (morph between conformations)

### 3. Structure Data Integration

**Sources**:
- PDB files bundled with database
- Fetch from RCSB PDB API
- AlphaFold predictions
- Local structure predictions

**Caching**: Cache fetched structures in IndexedDB

## Performance Considerations

3D rendering is GPU-intensive:
- Use instanced rendering for large structures
- LOD (Level of Detail) for distant views
- Throttle updates during rotation
- Quality presets for different devices

**Quality Presets**:
| Preset | Target | Settings |
|--------|--------|----------|
| Low | Mobile, old laptops | Low poly, no shadows |
| Medium | Average laptops | Medium detail, basic lighting |
| High | Desktop | Full detail, shadows |
| Ultra | Gaming PCs | Max detail, SSAO, anti-aliasing |

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: Phase 1 (database for structure references)
- **Blocks**: None (nice-to-have feature)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] 3D model loads and renders
- [ ] Can rotate/zoom/pan with mouse
- [ ] Multiple rendering modes available
- [ ] Fullscreen mode works
- [ ] Keyboard navigation in fullscreen
- [ ] Performance meets 30fps minimum

## Files to Create

```
packages/web/src/
├── components/
│   └── Model3DView.tsx              # NEW
├── visualization/
│   └── structure/
│       ├── StructureRenderer.ts     # NEW (Three.js wrapper)
│       ├── structureLoader.ts       # NEW (PDB/mmCIF parsing)
│       └── renderModes.ts           # NEW (ribbon, surface, etc.)
```

## Estimated Complexity: High

3D graphics programming is specialized.  {"id":"phage_explorer-owy","content_hash":"310f5ff4aa1a73c2dfb4d6d668fb4b7f1669e8e8ce567afdcfa904a2a60c08e0","title":"Layer 2: Host Range Predictor (CRISPR Spacer Matching)","description":"Fuse CRISPR spacer hits with receptor-binding motif classifiers. Score per host lineage with evidence (spacer alignments, RBP motif hits). MinHash/LSH index for spacers + HMMER for RBP families. Bayesian fusion scoring. Evidence table per host with strict/permissive thresholds, host cloud sized by probability. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## HOST-RANGE INFERENCE VIA CRISPR SPACERS + RECEPTOR MOTIFS

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Fuse CRISPR spacer hits with receptor-binding motif classifiers to predict host range. Score per host lineage and display evidence.

### How to Build

**Spacer DB Ingest:**
- Parse spacer FASTAs/TSVs from CRISPRdb, IMG/VR, CRISPR-Cas++
- Build MinHash/LSH index (Rust+WASM) for fast lookup
- Fall back to banded alignments for top hits
- Store spacer→host mappings with confidence

**Motif/RBP Typing:**
- HMMER precompute for RBP families (beta-helix, depolymerase, etc.)
- TS classifier on receptor-binding domain signatures
- Link RBP domains to known receptors (LPS, OmpA, FhuA, etc.)

**Fusion Scoring:**
```
log P(host | spacer, motif) = w₁ × spacer_score + w₂ × motif_score + prior
Confidence = σ(log_odds)
```

**Evidence Types:**
- Spacer alignments with mismatches
- RBP domain hits with E-values
- Known receptor associations

### TUI Features
- Host leaderboard with confidence bars
- Press host to see spacer hit list and RBP motif hits
- 'Host cloud' ASCII plot sized by probability
- Toggle 'strict vs permissive' thresholds
- Evidence drilldown per candidate

### Why It's Good
- Combines complementary evidence types
- Tightens weak host predictions
- Points to mechanisms (which receptor, which spacer)
- Guides experimental validation

### Implementation Stack
- Rust+WASM LSH for spacer queries
- HMMER precompute for RBP domains
- Fusion/scoring in TS
- SQLite cache for hits and scores
- Ink UI tables and plots

 {"id":"phage_explorer-pml","content_hash":"721b7f2054f5477cbfce8792c4f67521c95eda0bca8a071b6e26642f7a507793","title":"[WEB-P1] Data Layer: Browser SQLite & Offline Support","description":"# Phase 1: Data Layer (Target: Weeks 2-3)

## Purpose
Enable the web app to access the phage database and work completely offline. This phase bridges the gap between our SQLite-based TUI and the browser environment.

## Why This Matters
The phage database is the core data asset. Without efficient browser access to this data, the web app cannot function. Offline support means researchers can use the app in field conditions, on planes, or anywhere without connectivity.

## Strategic Approach
We use **sql.js** (SQLite compiled to WebAssembly) rather than a backend API because:
1. No server infrastructure to maintain
2. Works offline after initial load
3. Same database file as TUI (consistency)
4. Near-native query performance via WASM

## Deliverables
1. **sql.js Integration** - Browser adapter in `db-runtime` package
2. **Database Build Pipeline** - Compression, optimization, manifest
3. **IndexedDB Caching** - Store database locally for fast reload
4. **Service Worker** - Workbox-based offline support
5. **State Persistence** - Browser-safe Zustand hydration

## Technical Details

### Database Pipeline
```
phage.db (source) → Optimize → Brotli compress → Chunk → manifest.json
```
- Target compressed size: ~10-15MB (from ~50MB)
- Brotli quality 11 for maximum compression

### Caching Strategy
1. First visit: Fetch compressed DB from CDN
2. Decompress and initialize sql.js
3. Cache raw DB in IndexedDB (faster than re-decompress)
4. Subsequent visits: Load from IndexedDB
5. Background check for updates

### Service Worker Strategy
- **App Shell**: Precached at build time
- **Database**: StaleWhileRevalidate (use cached, update in background)
- **WASM modules**: CacheFirst (immutable)
- **Fonts**: CacheFirst with long TTL

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Database loads and queries work (same results as TUI)
- [ ] Second visit uses cached database (measurable speedup)
- [ ] Offline mode fully functional
- [ ] State persists across sessions
- [ ] Database update mechanism works
- [ ] Cold start with cached DB: <1s

## Risks & Mitigations
- **Risk**: sql.js performance for large queries
  - **Mitigation**: Precompute heavy queries, use prepared statements
- **Risk**: IndexedDB storage limits
  - **Mitigation**: Check quota, handle gracefully, compress if needed
- **Risk**: Safari quirks with SharedArrayBuffer
  - **Mitigation**: Feature detect, fall back to single-threaded mode

## Dependencies
- Phase 0 must be complete (app scaffold exists)
- Existing `phage.db` database file","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:56:12.864565-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T01:03:08.196808-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T01:03:08.196808-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-pml","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-87z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:04:29.513114-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-vru3","content_hash":"ce23fee61cc8443c5e7c20813a127d4d1eab7eb30158beec1f307738695f298d","title":"Feature: 3D Rendering Overhaul","description":"","notes":"# Feature: 3D Rendering Overhaul

## Executive Summary

A comprehensive overhaul of the 3D molecular structure viewer to address critical usability and visual quality issues.

## Current Problems

1. **Zoom-to-Extents Broken**: Molecules don't auto-fit to viewport (THIRD TIME REQUESTED)
2. **Poor Atom Coloring**: Ball-and-stick mode uses colors that are hard to distinguish
3. **Redundant Render Mode**: Cartoon mode is nearly identical to Ribbon
4. **Missing Phages**: Some catalog phages may not appear with 3D models

## Sub-Tasks

### P0 - Critical
- `phage_explorer-de4o`: Fix zoom-to-extents auto-fit

### P1 - High Priority
- `phage_explorer-qm08`: Implement CPK element coloring
- `phage_explorer-z49z`: Verify all phages have 3D models

### P2 - Normal Priority
- `phage_explorer-xkxf`: Remove redundant Cartoon mode
- `phage_explorer-k3l5`: Add functional group visualization

## Technical Context

**File Locations:**
- Model viewer: `/packages/web/src/components/Model3DView.tsx`
- Structure loader: `/packages/web/src/visualization/structure-loader.ts`
- Phage catalog: `/packages/data-pipeline/src/phage-catalog.ts`

**Key Technologies:**
- Three.js for WebGL rendering
- React Query for structure fetching
- RCSB PDB for structure data

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All molecules auto-fit to viewport on load
- [ ] Atoms clearly distinguishable by element type
- [ ] Only 3 render modes (Ball-Stick, Ribbon, Surface)
- [ ] All 12 catalog phages have working 3D models
- [ ] Functional groups highlighted in complex structures

## Definition of Done

All sub-tasks completed and tested on multiple phages across different device sizes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:34.423964-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:34:11.514737-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vru3","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-jjq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.298042-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","content_hash":"c88d05fa88fc226763f3d80921c80d361e93499d450fdd81fc0c516f0a9fe08d","title":"Pillar 5: Context-Aware Help System","description":"## Smart Explanations That Appear When Relevant

### Philosophy
Instead of requiring users to seek out help, context-aware explanations appear automatically based on what the user is viewing. This creates a 'just-in-time learning' experience.

### How It Works
1. **InfoButton Component** - Small [?] icons placed next to technical displays
2. **ContextPanel Component** - Slides in with relevant explanation
3. **useContextHelp Hook** - Determines what explanation to show based on current view

### Context Triggers

**When viewing DNA sequence:**
- What are these letters? (A, T, G, C)
- Why are some colored differently?
- What does the position number mean?

**When viewing amino acids:**
- How did DNA become amino acids?
- What do the colors represent?
- What is a reading frame?

**When viewing genes:**
- What is a gene/ORF?
- What do start/end positions mean?
- What is a locus tag?

**When using analysis overlays:**
- GC Skew: Why does GC content vary? What does skew indicate?
- Complexity: What is sequence entropy? Why does it matter?
- Repeats: What are direct/inverted repeats? Why important?
- HGT: What is horizontal gene transfer?
- CRISPR: What is CRISPR? How do spacers work?
- Synteny: What is gene order conservation?
- Dot Plot: How do I read this matrix?

### Implementation

**ContextPanel Component:**
- Slides in from right side
- Title, explanation text, diagrams
- Links to: glossary terms, full module, visualizer
- Dismiss button, auto-dismiss on navigation

**InfoButton Component:**
- Consistent [?] icon style
- Hover preview (short), click for full panel
- Only visible in Beginner Mode

**ContextProvider:**
- Wraps app, tracks current context
- Provides `showContext(topicId)` function
- Manages panel open/close state

### Content Structure
Each context help entry has:
- `id`: unique identifier
- `title`: what is this?
- `shortExplanation`: 1-2 sentences
- `fullExplanation`: detailed (2-3 paragraphs)
- `glossaryTerms`: linked terms
- `relatedModule`: link to foundational module
- `visualizer`: optional interactive element

### Files to Create
- packages/web/src/education/context/ContextPanel.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/context/ContextProvider.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/components/InfoButton.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/context/topics/*.ts (content files)
- packages/web/src/education/hooks/useContextHelp.ts

### Integration Points
- SequenceView: add info buttons for DNA/AA display
- GeneMapCanvas: add info for gene annotations
- Each overlay: add context explanation header
- ComparisonOverlay: explain metrics

### Why This Pillar Matters
This is the 'magic' of Beginner Mode—help appears exactly when needed without the user having to search. It transforms the app from 'tool for experts' to 'learning environment for everyone'.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.750332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.750332-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ty1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.751579-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-0weo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.753088-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
